segments.text: The Lord said to Job:
Will the one who contends with the Almighty correct him? Let him who accuses God answer him!
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then Job answered the Lord:
I am unworthy—how can I reply to you? I put my hand over my mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I spoke once, but I have no answer— twice, but I will say no more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then the Lord spoke to Job out of the storm:
Brace yourself like a man; I will question you, and you shall answer me.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Would you discredit my justice? Would you condemn me to justify yourself?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Do you have an arm like God's, and can your voice thunder like his?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then adorn yourself with glory and splendor, and clothe yourself in honor and majesty.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Unleash the fury of your wrath, look at all who are proud and bring them low,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: look at all who are proud and humble them, crush the wicked where they stand.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Bury them all in the dust together; shroud their faces in the grave.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then I myself will admit to you that your own right hand can save you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Look at Behemoth, which I made along with you and which feeds on grass like an ox.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: What strength it has in its loins, what power in the muscles of its belly!
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Its tail sways like a cedar; the sinews of its thighs are close-knit.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Its bones are tubes of bronze, its limbs like rods of iron.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: It ranks first among the works of God, yet its Maker can approach it with his sword.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The hills bring it their produce, and all the wild animals play nearby.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Under the lotus plants it lies, hidden among the reeds in the marsh.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The lotuses conceal it in their shadow; the poplars by the stream surround it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: A raging river does not alarm it; it is secure, though the Jordan should surge against its mouth.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Can anyone capture it by the eyes, or trap it and pierce its nose?
segments.speaker: The Lord
